mappings.rationaleAI: Population study addresses determinants (A.1) including fertility (A.1.a), mortality (A.1.b), and migration (A.1.c). Demographic transition addresses historical changes (A.2). Future challenges address projections (A.5).
Malthus's Essay directly addresses Malthusian theory (A.3.d).
Population control addresses fertility (A.1.a) and governmental policies (A.4).
Migration flows address migration and refugee movements (A.1.c).
Forced migration addresses migration movements (A.1.c).
Migration and displacement address migration movements (A.1.c).
Disease patterns address mortality and longevity (A.1.b).
Population health addresses mortality and longevity (A.1.b).
Pandemics address mortality (A.1.b) and historical changes (A.2).
Urban planning addresses planning patterns (B.3), methods (B.3.a), social aspects (B.3.b), and urban growth (B.4.e).
Suburban development addresses suburbanization (B.4.b).
Urban segregation addresses urbanization (B.1) and urban growth problems (B.4.e).
Architecture addresses methods of urban planning (B.3.a).
Architectural innovations address methods of planning (B.3.a).
Parks and urban environments address planning methods (B.3.a) and social aspects (B.3.b).
Industrialization addresses the history of urbanization (B.2).

mappings.vsiAuthor: John A. Matthews, David T. Herbert
mappings.rationaleAI: Human geography addresses population (A), urbanization (B.1), and regional integration (B.4.c).
Spatial processes address urbanization (B.1) and regional integration (B.4.c).
Climate change addresses ecological theories (A.3.g) and environmental change (B.4.f).
Environmental protection addresses ecological theories (A.3.g) and environmental change (B.4.f).
Pollution control addresses environmental change (B.4.f).
Environmental ethics address ecological theories (A.3.g) and environmental change (B.4.f).
Sustainability addresses ecological theories (A.3.g) and environmental change (B.4.f).
Population dynamics address ecological theories of population and conservation (A.3.g).
Humanity's geological impact addresses ecological theories (A.3.g) and environmental change (B.4.f).
Mass migration addresses migration (A.1.c). Technologies address urban dominance (B.4.d).
Geographic factors address regional integration (B.4.c).
Social institutions address urbanization characteristics (B.1).
Borders address migration (A.1.c) and regional integration (B.4.c).
Futures studies address population projections (A.5).
